text: Defense counsel in a wage-and-hour class action admitted using LexisNexis's AI tool to help construct submissions without reviewing them for correctness, resulting in AI-hallucinated citations. The court imposed $1,710 in attorneys' fees (AI-authority-related) and a $1,000 monetary penalty on defense counsel under Rule 37, 28 U.S.C. § 1927, and inherent powers. A separate $46,980 discovery sanction was also imposed on defendants for unrelated witness-declaration misconduct.
deployer: Defense Counsel for Sushi Katsuei (unnamed in opinion)
canonical_source_conflicted: Genuine federal employment/wage-and-hour caselaw; court submission standards
mitigation_gap: Counsel used LexisNexis AI to draft court submissions without reviewing AI output for accuracy before filing.
reliance_or_harm: $1,710 AI-attributable attorneys' fees + $1,000 monetary penalty = $2,710 on defense counsel; fabricated citations submitted in class-action proceedings.
